rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARTHUR LAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/24/21. No earlier availability. If the statutory fee is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] Costs incurred by the attorney must clearly indicate what was filed or sent for which filing fees or courier charges were incurred. E-filing charges are not ordinarily $35 each.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HUSAM MOUSA QANA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Record
rulings.Rulings: moved to 2pm, the time approved by rosie, even though the caption said 9 am. This tentative ruling is entered by Judge Ross C. Moody. The Petitioner's Motion to Seal Proposed Fee Agreement is denied. For the purposes of deciding whether to seal court records, the party seeking the sealing of the documents bears the burden of presenting information sufficient to identify the specific information claimed to be entitled to such treatment and to identify the nature of the harm threatened by disclosure. (H.B. Fuller Co. v. Doe (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 879, 894.) The party must set forth specific facts in support of a motion to seal; statements that are too conclusory, abstract, or generic are not sufficient to satisfy the burden. (Id. at 895-896.) If specific facts have not been enumerated demonstrating the harm that would result if the documents are made part of the public record, then there is no basis for the court to conclude that the interest in keeping the records confidential overrides the public's interest in having access to court records. (Id. at 896-898.) A court may order that a record be filed under seal only if it expressly finds facts that establish: (1) an overriding interest exists that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the records; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and, (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. (CRC 2.550(d); NBC Subsidiary (KNBC-TV), Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (Locke) (1999) 20 Cal.4th 1178, 1216-1218). The instant motion does not set forth specific facts to support each of these five elements. Accordingly, it is denied. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on February 23, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.

Petition will be reviewed once Notice of Petition to Administer Estate, Proof of Publication, and Supplement to Duties and Liabilities (DE-147S, which cannot be e-filed and must have a cover sheet attached) are filed. Filed stamped copy to be provided to Room 202 at least two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ISABEL CASH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Richard Cash and Eileen Cash have unknown addresses. Declaration of Due Diligence filed 01/27/2021. 2) The will is not self-proving as it does not contain the attestation language required by Probate Code 8220, 6240.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZENA WERB
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing solely to admit will. No appearance required. No conformed copy of signed order will be returned (no self-addressed stamped envelope provided). Bond modified to $301,000 with consent of counsel. Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by May 24, 2022. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for June 8, 2022.
